basics.name: Michael Doyle
basics.label: Senior AI Research Engineer
basics.summary: A machine learning researcher with a passion for language, open source, and applying research to important problems.

work.name: The MITRE Corporation
work.summary: Led multiple ML projects. Researched, developed, tested, trained, and deployed machine learning models and applications.
highlights: Maintained internal GPU servers and services
Directly deployed trained models on government sponsor systems

work.name: Space Micro Inc.
work.summary: Wrote, simulated, synthesized, and implemented FPGA code in VHDL using ModelSim, Synplify Pro, Vivado, and Libero Designer. Designed a PCB with KiCAD and manufactured EMI-shielded housing.

publications.name: Reinforcement Learning for Wildfire Mitigation in Simulated Disaster Environments
publications.summary: This paper presents a reinforcement learning approach to wildfire mitigation in simulated disaster environments. We release two software libraries, SimFire and SimHarness, to facilitate future research in this area. The former is a wildfire simulator, and the latter is the reinforcement learning library.

publications.name: Practical Attacks on Machine Translation using Paraphrase
publications.publisher: Association for Machine Translation in the Americas (AMTA) 2022
publications.summary: This research investigated the vulnerability of machine translation systems to adversarial attacks constructed with limited information. A novel attack method was proposed that generates perturbations using paraphrases and evaluates their impact on meaning preservation and translation degradation across various language pairs and systems.

publications.name: The vulnerability of UAVs: an adversarial machine learning perspective
publications.summary: This research proposes a methodology to evaluate the vulnerability of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to adversarial machine learning attacks by analyzing potential attack vectors at each stage of UAV operation and assessing the feasibility of both established and novel attack methods.

publications.name: Testing the Effect of Code Documentation on Large Language Model Code Understanding
publications.publisher: North American Association for Computational Linguistics (NAACL) 2024
publications.summary: This research empirically analyzes how code documentation quality impacts the code generation and understanding capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). It reveals that incorrect documentation significantly hinders LLMs' code comprehension, while incomplete or missing documentation has no significant impact.
